title: Paspalum oreophilum
intro: Paspalum oreophilum is a perennial grass species native to South America, traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to exhibit potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can be beneficial in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of P. oreophilum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. P. oreophilum is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
taxonomy: Paspalum oreophilum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Panicoideae and tribe Paniceae. Paspalum oreophilum is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. This species is part of the genus Paspalum, which includes around 200 species of grasses. Paspalum oreophilum is further classified as Paspalum oreophilum (Stieber & Zuloaga), a synonym of Paspalum virgatum.
